headline: Meta cloud threat hits AI infrastructure names
detail: Meta positioning as a cloud competitor directly pressures companies selling AI compute capacity. For your book, this is a sentiment headwind for the whole chip/AI infrastructure complex - SMH and SOXL are the most exposed. If this narrative builds, rotation out of chips could accelerate.
rec: No action on a new buy - but watch SMH and SOXL closely. If SMH breaks toward $580, your SOXL stop at $200 becomes urgent. No scanner setup in chips is strong enough today to justify adding into this news.

headline: Sandisk and Micron sink as rotation trade builds
detail: Memory chip names selling off on rotation confirms the broader chip weakness isn't just one stock. This is direct sector pressure on SMH and by extension SOXL. Supply shortage caveat may limit how far losses go, but momentum is negative right now.
rec: No action to add. This headline reinforces holding your SOXL and SMH stops firmly - rotation trades can run further than expected, especially with margin amplifying your downside.

headline: ServiceNow and Salesforce look like buys, analyst says
detail: An analyst calling NOW and CRM oversold suggests enterprise software may be bottoming while chips rotate out. NOW appears on the scanner as a WATCH at 8/12 with entry 105.69-105.59 and stop 104.82, but daily bias is down - grade B at best.
rec: Worth monitoring NOW near 105.59-105.69 with stop 104.82, but only if you have dry powder and are not adding margin. The analyst call plus scanner setup is a mild reason to watch - not a strong reason to buy today with chips under pressure dominating your book.

headline: Is Meta giving up on cutting-edge AI? Wall Street divided
detail: Uncertainty around Meta's AI direction creates noise across the AI/chip supply chain. Divided Wall Street opinion means volatility, not clarity. Your leveraged chip ETFs hate uncertainty - it creates whipsaw that decay exploits.
rec: No action. Uncertainty headlines are the worst environment for leveraged ETFs like SOXL and SPAL. Stay defensive, respect your stops, and let this story clarify before adding any exposure.
